summary: Soundboard capture of a wildly energetic New Year’s‑run show – superb Jerry, hot Phil and a legendary Morning Dew, though the mix drops out at peak moments.
review: The performance is celebrated for its ecstatic second set, especially the “Morning Dew” jam that many reviewers call divine, plus an inspired “Playing in the Band”, a fiery “Cold Rain > Greatest” and a surprise Clarence Clemons‑enhanced “Iko”.  The soundboard source is generally clear and powerful, but several listeners note occasional volume loss on Garcia’s solo and a brief mix glitch during Dew, suggesting a good‑but‑not‑perfect recording.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical peaks (especially Dew and the second‑set jams) while noting only occasional mix issues; this yields a high show score offset by a solid but imperfect recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, powerful mix overall; however listeners report a brief dropout/volume loss on Garcia’s solo during Morning Dew and occasional mix glitches at peak moments.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and standard, while the second set erupts into high‑energy improvisations that build to a double encore; the flow is praised for its escalating intensity and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described dancing in front of his mic, playing with showman’s joy; his solo on Dew momentarily drops out of the mix but overall his spirit is infectious.
band_member_comments.Phil: Hot and “blasting” throughout – especially on Cold Rain and the bass‑bombs; his new Ken Smith bass is prominent and praised for its tone.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) steps in during Dew when Garcia’s volume dips, pushing the jam harder and adding extra intensity.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Healy occasionally loses Jerry in the mix at peak moments but otherwise the rhythm section is described as “firing on all cylinders”.
